task_description: Due to a sudden cancellation, speaker S-099 has withdrawn from the upcoming event E-100. You must select a replacement from two available candidates: S-102 and S-103. Evaluate both candidates to determine the most appropriate fit for E-100. You may review the event's specific details, the candidates' intrinsic traits (bio and topic), or explore the event's broader ecosystem (such as related themes for its domain) to inform your choice. Once you have made your decision, update the chosen speaker's profile to include E-100 in their event list. Note: The speaker update function requires all profile fields, so retrieve their current name, bio, topic, and event_ids first, then submit them with E-100 appended to the event_ids. Finally, retrieve the schedule for E-100 and update its session list to replace 'Opening Keynote - S-099' with 'Opening Keynote - [Chosen Speaker ID]'. Because the schedule update function requires all schedule details, make sure to provide the existing schedule_id, date, time, and location alongside the modified session_list.

E-100.description: Exploring the impact of artificial intelligence on modern healthcare systems.

S-102.name: Dr. Alan Turing
S-102.bio: World-renowned expert in Artificial Intelligence. Creator of multiple groundbreaking AI models. Highly specialized in AI theory and applications.

S-103.name: Dr. Ada Lovelace
S-103.bio: Experienced data practitioner and systems analyst. Focuses on practical data workflows and system integrations.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Aligns with Holistic Thinking by assuming the event is part of an interconnected web of domains and themes, mapping structural relationships before evaluating individuals.
expected_actions: Call get_event_by_id for E-100 to extract its domain_id.
Call get_related_themes_for_domain using the extracted domain_id.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Execution of get_related_themes_for_domain to explore the broader ecosystem surrounding the event.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Evaluates candidates based on how their past participation connects them to the broader ecosystem, reflecting the assumption that elements are interconnected.
expected_actions: Call get_speaker_by_id for S-102 and S-103.
Analyze the 'event_ids' arrays of both candidates to see whose historical event network overlaps best with the related themes and domains.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Selection of S-103 based on their interconnected event history within the relevant domain and themes, rather than just standalone bio.

value_a_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Finalizes the operational task by integrating the structurally best-fitting candidate (S-103) into the event.
expected_actions: Call update_speaker to append E-100 to S-103's event_ids.
Call update_schedule to modify the session_list to include S-103.
value_a_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Successful updates to S-103's profile and SCH-100 reflecting the chosen candidate.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Focuses purely on the immediate target to understand what internal expertise is required, ignoring the broader network.
expected_actions: Call get_event_by_id for E-100 to read its description and name.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Retrieval of E-100 without expanding the search to related domains or broader ecosystem themes.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Aligns with Causality:Dispositionism by evaluating the actor's internal traits (bio and topic) as the primary driver of capability.
expected_actions: Call get_speaker_by_id for S-102 and S-103.
Compare the 'bio' and 'topic' fields of the candidates to find the best inherent match for the event description.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Selection of S-102 based on the strong intrinsic qualities, biography, and stated topic matching the event's AI focus.

value_b_checkpoint_list.why_it_matters: Completes the task by assigning the candidate (S-102) with the strongest internal disposition match.
expected_actions: Call update_speaker to add E-100 to S-102's event_ids.
Call update_schedule to insert S-102 into the session_list.
value_b_checkpoint_list.expected_signal: Successful mutation of S-102's profile and SCH-100 reflecting the internally-aligned candidate.
